listOfQuestions.question: Which Microbiology concept is described by this clue: An organism group that includes yeasts and molds?
Which Microbiology concept is described by this clue: A single-celled eukaryotic microorganism?
Which Microbiology concept is described by this clue: The community of microorganisms living in an environment?
Which Microbiology concept is described by this clue: A microbe or agent that can cause disease?
Which Microbiology concept is described by this clue: A drug used to treat bacterial infections?
Which Microbiology concept is described by this clue: The ability of bacteria to survive an antibiotic?
Which Microbiology concept is described by this clue: Microbes grown under controlled conditions?
Which Microbiology concept is described by this clue: A shallow dish used to grow microorganisms?
Which Microbiology concept is described by this clue: Methods used to prevent unwanted contamination?
Which Microbiology concept is described by this clue: Destroying or removing all living microbes?
Which Microbiology concept is described by this clue: A chemical used to reduce microbes on surfaces?
Which Microbiology concept is described by this clue: A staining method that helps classify bacteria?
Which Microbiology concept is described by this clue: A visible group of microbes growing from cells?
Which Microbiology concept is described by this clue: Microbial metabolism that produces useful products without oxygen?
Which Microbiology concept is described by this clue: A single-celled fungus often used in baking and brewing?
Which Microbiology concept is described by this clue: The protein coat around a virus?
Which Microbiology concept is described by this clue: A cell used by a virus or microbe to reproduce?
Which Microbiology concept is described by this clue: A surface-attached community of microorganisms?
Which Microbiology concept is described by this clue: A single-celled organism without a nucleus?
Which Microbiology concept is described by this clue: An infectious particle that replicates inside host cells?
